title: Human Islets - RT Barcoding
author: Opentrons
description: For detailed protocol steps, please see below. The trash will be used as a waste reservoir for supernatant removal. This protocol is for full plates (96 samples) exclusively. Well 11 of the reservoir will be used for pooled samples.
steps_raw: For detailed protocol steps, please see below. The trash will be used as a waste reservoir for supernatant removal. This protocol is for full plates (96 samples) exclusively. Well 11 of the reservoir will be used for pooled samples.
To each of the well of the 96-well plate, add 1 μl of uniquely indexed oligo-dT (25 μM).
Incubate at 72 oC for 10 min and place the plate on ice.
To each well, add 2 μl Smart-seq3 RT mix (62.5 mM Tris-HCl (pH8.3), 75 mM NaCl, 6.25 mM MaCl2, 2.5 mM GTP, 20 mM DTT, 1.25 U/ μl RNase inhibitor, 5 U/ μl Maxima H-minus RT enzyme).
Incubate plate in a thermo cycler (off deck).
After reaction, add 60 μl of wash-resuspension buffer into each well.

Pool all wells into one reagent reservoir column by column. Wash the first 8-well column with 100μl of wash-resuspension buffer. Pipette up and down. Dispense 50 μl of cell suspension to the reagent reservoir. Transfer the remaining volume ( ~ 100 μl ) from the previous well to the well in the next column.
For the last column, transfer the entire volume to the reagent reservoir.
Repeat steps 6-7 for an additional two times.
Transfer the cell suspension mixture from the reagent reservoir to a 2 ml LoBind tube.
Spin cell down at 800g for 6 min.
Wash cells once with 500 μl of PBS+0.04%BSA. Spin cell down at 800g for 6 min. Aspirate supernatant.

Measure the residual liquid volume in the epp tube. Resuspend cells with the residual volume and transfer all solution into one tube of a 8-tube strip. Wash the epp tube with (80– residual volume) μl of PBS+0.04%BSA buffer and transfer all liquid into the same tube in the 8-tube strip.
materials_raw: Opentrons 96 Well Aluminum Block with Generic PCR Strip 200 µL
